name: Samuel Barber
abstract: Samuel Osmond Barber II (March 9, 1910 – January 23, 1981) was an American composer, pianist, conductor, baritone, and music educator, and one of the most celebrated composers of the mid-20th century. Principally influenced by nine years' composition studies with Rosario Scalero at the Curtis Institute and more than 25 years' study with …

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: But still the emotional responses are pretty much the same, whether it’s adulation or whether it’s a joy or sadness, a tear, jumping up crazy, whatever, a big smile on your face, whatever. It’s a fact that whether Samuel Barber “Adagio for Strings” or John Coltrane doing “Greensleeves”...
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Oliver Nelson Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: he could have dedicated it to something. But he called it “Sound Pieces,” just like Samuel Barber called that beautiful piece “Adagio for Strings.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Oliver Nelson Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 10, 2020
